dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9722 IH: Fair Treatment of Religious Organizations Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moore of Utah (for himself, Mr. Cline, Ms. Tenney, Mr. Owens, Mr. Moran, Mr. Grothman, Mr. Kennedy of Utah, and Ms. Maloy) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to ensure fair treatment of certain charitable organizations.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fair Treatment of Religious Organizations Act of 2026.

section.header: Determination of religious purpose for purposes of tax-exempt status

subsection.text: Section 501 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

paragraph.text: a religious belief or practice concerning marriage, sexuality, or gender identity shall not be treated as being inconsistent with law or public policy, and
a belief shall not fail to be treated as a religious belief merely because such belief is not compelled by or central to a system of religion.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
